g.data-narration: Welcome to System Programs. These utilities define what users perceive as the operating system.
System programs are utilities shipped with the OS that sit between the kernel and applications.
g.text: Programs shipped with the OS (not part of kernel) that provide a convenient
environment for program development and execution. They define most of
the user's view of the operating system, sitting between apps and the kernel.

g.data-narration: The OS has three layers: Applications at top, System Programs in middle, Kernel at core.
File Management programs handle creating, copying, moving, deleting files and directories.
g.text: ls, cp, mv, rm, mkdir, rmdir, find, chmod

g.data-narration: Status Information programs query the system for date, time, memory, disk space, and user info.
g.text: date, df, du, free, ps, top, uname, who

g.data-narration: File Modification tools allow creating and editing file contents - editors, search, and transformation.
g.text: vi, vim, nano, sed, grep, awk, diff

g.data-narration: Programming support tools include compilers, debuggers, interpreters, and build automation.
g.text: gcc, javac, gdb, python, make, ld, as

g.data-narration: Program Loading tools handle loading executables into memory - loaders, linkers, and debuggers.
Communications programs enable remote login, file transfer, email, and web browsing across networks.
g.text: ssh, ftp, scp, wget, curl, mail

g.data-narration: Background services (daemons) run continuously at system boot providing system-wide services.
g.text: httpd, sshd, cron, syslogd, systemd - run at boot, no user interaction

g.data-narration: System programs differ from applications: they come with OS, serve developers, and directly use system calls.
Seven categories of system programs: File Management, Status Info, File Modification, Programming Support, Loading, Communications, and Daemons.
g.text: 💡 Exam Tip: Memorize all 7 categories with 2 examples each

g.data-narration: System programs provide the user interface to the OS. Users interact with utilities like ls, not the kernel directly.
g.text: Key Takeaway: Users perceive the OS through system programs, not through system calls or kernel
Understanding system programs is essential for OS architecture comprehension
